_meta.source: iRacing official manual V1 (March 2024), garage screenshots, iGPFun F317 guide, community data
notes: Values marked 'confirmed' are read directly from the official manual garage screenshots.
Values marked 'estimated' are derived from the F317 predecessor, iRacing conventions for similar cars, or community sources.
iRacing garage uses imperial units internally (inches, lbs/in, deg, psi). Metric display is a UI toggle.
The garage enforces ranges via sliders — values outside these ranges cannot be entered.
Tech inspection additionally checks ride height and aero package compatibility.
Front springs are torsion bars (adjusted by outer diameter). Rear springs are coil springs (adjusted by rate).
Rear spring rates must be symmetrical (changed in pairs) per homologation rules.
_meta.confidence: partial — exact slider endpoints require in-game verification

tire_type.notes: Dry = slick, Wet = treaded. Selected per session conditions.

cold_pressure.notes: Per-corner adjustment. Baseline is 18.0 psi (124 kPa) all corners. iRacing enforces a minimum cold pressure (historically ~15 psi for open-wheel). Hot pressures typically build to 130-140 kPa.

aero_package.notes: Changes wing components and their setting limits. Changing this may alter available front flap configs and angle ranges.

front_flap_config.notes: Depends on aero package. High DF aero package requires High DF flap. Low DF requires Low DF flap. Medium DF allows either.

front_flap_angle.notes: Adjusts front wing upper flap elements. Higher = more front downforce, more drag, aero forward.
front_flap_angle.confidence: baseline confirmed, range estimated from similar iRacing open-wheel cars

front_flap_gurney.notes: Wicker added to trailing edge of front wing. Significantly increases front downforce and drag. Large aero balance shift forward.

rear_upper_flap.notes: Main rear wing element. Primary tool for overall downforce level and aero balance. Higher = more rear downforce, more drag.

rear_beam_wing.notes: Lower rear wing element above axle. Higher = more overall downforce, aero shifts rearward.

heave_spring.notes: Front third spring. Handles vertical loads without affecting roll. 0 = disconnected. Primarily controls aero platform consistency.

heave_perch_offset.notes: Preloads heave spring. Decreasing = more preload, adds weight to corner, increases ride height. Increasing = opposite.

pushrod_length_offset.notes: Adjusts front ride height without affecting spring preload. Positive = raises ride height, negative = lowers. Set after springs and heave spring are configured.

arb_size.notes: Front anti-roll bar diameter. Disconnected removes ARB entirely (reduces understeer but hurts aero in high-speed).

arb_arm_length.notes: Longer arms = softer ARB assembly, less roll stiffness. Shorter = stiffer, more understeer.

arb_blades.notes: Blade orientation. 1 = softest, 5 = stiffest. Adjustable in-car via F8 black box 'FARB' setting.
arb_blades.confidence: confirmed (range 1-5 stated in manual text, baseline visible)

ride_height.notes: Reference point on chassis to ground. Lower = more aero but risk of bottoming. iGPFun recommends front ~15-20mm. Manual says 'run front as low as possible'. Tech inspection enforces a minimum.
ride_height.confidence: baseline confirmed, range estimated from iGPFun guide

torsion_bar_preload.notes: Preload on front torsion bar. Negative = more preload, more weight on corner, higher ride height at that corner. Adjust in pairs (L/R) to avoid crossweight changes.

torsion_bar_od.notes: Front torsion bar outer diameter = front spring stiffness. Larger = stiffer. Stiffness proportional to d^4. Per-corner adjustment.
torsion_bar_od.confidence: baseline confirmed, range estimated from F317/similar cars

comp_damping.notes: Front compression damping. Higher = more resistance to compression, faster weight transfer, more understeer.
comp_damping.confidence: baseline confirmed, range estimated from similar iRacing cars

rbd_damping.notes: Front rebound damping. Higher = more resistance to extension, better aero control but can cause wheel to lose contact.

camber.baseline_note: Value not fully visible in manual screenshot, but rear shows -2.4 deg
camber.notes: Negative camber desired on all four wheels. More negative = more cornering force but less braking grip. Per-corner adjustment.
camber.confidence: range estimated, typical values from community

toe_in.baseline_note: Displayed as fraction of inches. -2/32 = toe-out
toe_in.notes: Negative = toe-out (faster turn-in response, less stable straight). Positive = toe-in (more stable, sluggish response). Per-corner.

spring_perch_offset.notes: Rear spring preload. Decreasing = more preload, more weight on corner, higher ride height. Adjust in pairs.

spring_rate.notes: Rear coil spring rate. Must be symmetrical (L/R same). Stiffer = better aero platform, less mechanical grip. Softer = more grip, more body roll.

comp_damping.notes: Rear compression damping. Higher = more resistance, more understeer on throttle application.

camber.notes: Rear camber. More negative = more cornering stability, less braking stability. Per-corner adjustment.

toe_in.notes: Positive = toe-in (more straight-line stability). Negative = toe-out (better direction change). Per-corner.

ride_height.notes: Rear ride height. Higher than front creates positive rake. iGPFun recommends rear 10-20mm higher than front.

third_spring.notes: Rear third/heave spring. Controls vertical movement without affecting roll. 0 = disconnected. Important for controlling rear ride height under aero load.

third_perch_offset.notes: Rear third spring preload. Adjusts overall rear ride height.

pushrod_length_offset.notes: Adjusts rear ride height without affecting spring preload. Positive = raises, negative = lowers.

arb_size.notes: Rear anti-roll bar. Affects rear roll stiffness and oversteer/understeer balance.

arb_arm_length.notes: Rear ARB arm length. Higher positions may represent longer/softer arms.
arb_arm_length.confidence: baseline confirmed, position naming from screenshot

front_master_cylinder.notes: Larger = less front line pressure = rearward bias shift, more pedal effort. Smaller = more front pressure = forward bias shift.

rear_master_cylinder.notes: Larger = less rear line pressure. Smaller = more rear pressure.

brake_pressure_bias.notes: Percentage of brake pressure to front wheels. Adjustable in-car. Manual says typical 52-58% (from engineer.py system prompt). Braking Lab recommends 56-58% starting point.

cross_weight.notes: Percentage of weight on RF+LR diagonal. 50% = balanced. Higher = understeer in LH turns, oversteer in RH. Adjusted indirectly via spring preloads.

gear_stack.notes: Five fixed gear stack options. Shorter = better acceleration, lower top speed. Longer = higher top speed, less acceleration. Top speed shown in garage (baseline shows 163.0 mph).
gear_stack.confidence: confirmed (5 options stated in manual text, baseline visible)

ramp_angles.notes: Lower angle = more locking force. Coast (decel): lower = more understeer. Power (accel): lower = more oversteer on exit, less understeer. Values are independent.

clutch_friction_faces.notes: Multiplies locking force. More faces = more locking. Higher = more understeer off-throttle entering corners, more oversteer on exit.

preload.notes: Static locking force always present. More preload = more understeer under braking, more oversteer on throttle. Less = opposite.

tech_inspection_notes: Setup must pass tech inspection for the selected track type.
Baseline setup passes all tracks. Track-specific setups are fitted for their track category.
Loading a setup from a different track type may fail inspection — usually ride height is the issue.
If tech fails, adjust ride height using P-rod Length Offset at either end.
Positive P-rod offset = raises ride height; negative = lowers it.
Aero package compatibility: High DF flap cannot be used with Low DF package and vice versa.
Rear spring rates must be symmetrical (same L/R).

baseline_setup_summary.description: Default 'baseline' setup values read from official manual screenshots (Fuji baseline, imperial units)
